text.title: AI IaC Review Gate Core Rules
text.summary: Five testable rules every AI-IaC review gate enforces. Hard rules; CI MUST refuse merge when any check fails without an explicit waiver.

rule.statement: Every IaC PR touched by an AI assistant MUST execute all four checks: drift (provider-schema validation + plan-only diff), security (tfsec / kubesec / hadolint + posture-bound rules), performance (resource requests/limits, instance class, sync-in-async detection), multi-arch (build on every architecture in `arch-matrix.yaml`). Skipping a check requires an explicit waiver entry, never a silent pass.
rule.rationale: The four checks cover the dominant prod-breaking shapes. Three-check gates miss multi-arch (most common failure: arm64 not in the build matrix); four-check gates catch >95% of postmortem-classified failures.

rule.statement: The gate MUST run terraform / kubectl / helm in plan-only / dry-run mode. Apply / install in the gate is FORBIDDEN. CI credentials MUST be scoped to read + plan, never write. State files MUST be remote (S3, GCS, Terraform Cloud); local state in CI is FORBIDDEN.
rule.rationale: An apply-from-PR-CI gate destroys prod when the AI generates a destructive diff (`terraform destroy` lookalike). Plan-only is the only safe execution boundary; the gate's job is verification, not deployment.

rule.statement: A check waiver MUST include: (a) the check ID; (b) a written rationale >= 80 characters; (c) an expiry date <= 90 days out; (d) a named human approver from CODEOWNERS. Waivers without all four fields are REJECTED. Expired waivers re-block the merge automatically.
rule.rationale: Open-ended waivers become permanent exceptions. Bounded waivers with rationale + expiry produce a tracked debt list rather than a silent rules graveyard.

rule.statement: Docker builds and Helm chart container references MUST build successfully on EVERY architecture listed in `arch-matrix.yaml` (minimum: linux/amd64, linux/arm64). A `--platform amd64` only build is REJECTED unless `arch-matrix.yaml` declares amd64 as the sole architecture (with rationale).

rationale.source: ARM transition data; Graviton / Apple Silicon adoption

rule.rationale: AI assistants default to amd64 because most training examples were amd64. Modern fleets are mixed arch (Graviton in AWS, Apple Silicon for dev). Multi-arch build is the cheapest gate against "works on my laptop / fails on Graviton" incidents.

rule.statement: A `block`-severity finding MUST receive either a fix (re-run the gate) or a waiver (with all four fields per r3). The AI assistant CANNOT self-waive; a CODEOWNERS human is required. The gate's verdict is the merge gate; CI MUST refuse merge until verdict is pass or all blocks waived.
rule.rationale: If the AI can waive its own gate findings, the gate is theatre. CODEOWNERS protection is the structural defence; cultural rules alone are insufficient.

example.title: Multi-arch caught at gate, AI repairs
example.code:
# AI draft (FAIL multi-arch) FROM python:3.12-slim RUN apt-get install -y libpq-dev gcc # fails on arm64 base ABI
# Repair after gate feedback FROM --platform=$BUILDPLATFORM python:3.12-slim AS build ARG TARGETARCH RUN case "$TARGETARCH" in \ amd64|arm64) apt-get update && apt-get install -y libpq-dev gcc ;; \ *) echo "unsupported arch: $TARGETARCH" && exit 1 ;; \ esac
example.note: r4 satisfied: explicit arch branching, supported-arch list matches arch-matrix.yaml.

example.title: Apply-from-PR-CI
example.code: # CI job (FORBIDDEN per r2) steps: - name: deploy run: terraform apply -auto-approve
example.note: r2 violation. `apply -auto-approve` from PR CI on AI-generated diff is one of the highest-blast-radius patterns. The gate runs `plan`; apply is a separate, gated workflow with human approval.

example.title: Self-waiver by AI
example.code: Gate finding: SEC-003 root container, severity=block Waiver added by claude-code commit: reason: "not a production container" expiry: (none) approver: claude-code
example.note: r3 + r5 violations. Approver is the AI itself; no expiry. CI MUST reject the waiver.
